usage: Unity displays the matching station entry when the avatar enters its info trigger.

S01.title: S01 — Loading Station
S01.summary: The line's infeed: bulk components are kitted onto trays and released, one kit per cycle, to assembly.
decision_tree: 480 units/day @ 60 s → single-shift semi-automated line
Manual kitting is flexible & low-risk → open bench, no guarding
Must not starve assembly → powered buffered outfeed conveyor
Operator present → e-stop + stack light + floor markings
engineering_notes: 0.90 m ergonomic work height; angled bins within a 0.5 m reach arc
Powered roller outfeed at 0.40 m belt height feeds S02
Line head: no input conveyor, one output (C01)

S02.title: S02 — Assisted Assembly Station
S02.summary: Operators build the pump with pick-to-light guidance and torque-controlled tools before robotic handling.
decision_tree: Dexterous assembly → keep human operators
Error-sensitive medical build → pick-to-light + torque tools + HMI guidance
Two operators to hold 60 s cycle → 2.5 m bench
Manual, low-risk → open bench with e-stop
engineering_notes: Guided assembly with pick-to-light bins and an HMI step display
Feeds the robot cell (S03) via C02
Two-operator ergonomic layout at 0.90 m

S03.title: S03 — Robot Handling Cell
S03.summary: A fenced 6-axis robot closes the housing and reorients the pump — the automated hinge between manual assembly and inspection.
decision_tree: Repetitive precise handling → industrial robot
Moving robot = hazard → full fence + interlocked gate + keep-out zone
Robot reach 0.9 m + clearance → 3.0 m fenced cell
Automated zone starts here → decouples manual upstream from inspection
engineering_notes: Robot built from reusable assets in a true kinematic chain (Base→…→Gripper)
Only station requiring full guarding; working-envelope exported as a Unity trigger
Hero station — ≈12 k triangles, Unity-ready

S04.title: S04 — Vision Inspection Station
S04.summary: Cameras check assembly correctness and label quality; failed units are diverted automatically.
decision_tree: Objective defect detection needed → machine vision, not manual
Non-contact + fast → cameras + controlled lighting
Catch defects early → placed before functional test
Enclosed optics, low risk → e-stop + stack light, no fence
engineering_notes: Camera/lighting mast drives the taller 2.5 m envelope
Automatic reject diversion; ~20 s non-bottleneck cycle
Feeds functional test (S05) via C04

S05.title: S05 — Functional Test Station
S05.summary: Each pump is powered up and its flow rate verified; results are logged per serial number.
decision_tree: Medical device → 100 % functional + flow-rate test
Traceability required → per-serial test record via barcode
Test only good units → placed after vision
Enclosed fixture, low risk → e-stop + stack light
engineering_notes: Per-serial test logging supports medical traceability
~45 s automated test; failed units diverted
Feeds packaging (S06) via C05

S06.title: S06 — Packaging Station
S06.summary: The pump is boxed with its documentation, labeled and sealed for shipment.
decision_tree: Variable cartoning + docs → keep a human operator
Labeling/sealing repetitive → automate those steps
End-of-line dispatch prep → placed before storage
Manual, low-risk → open bench with e-stop
engineering_notes: Semi-automated: manual insertion, automated label/seal
~55 s cycle; feeds finished-goods storage via C06
Pallet staged for outbound handoff

S07.title: S07 — Finished Goods Storage
S07.summary: Sealed cartons accumulate on racks, ready for palletizing and dispatch.
decision_tree: Line runs continuously, dispatch in batches → buffer store
Accumulation, not processing → minimal automation
One shift of output → largest (3.5 m) footprint
Unmanned → stack light only, no e-stop required
engineering_notes: Buffer sized for ~one shift of finished goods
Unmanned: e-stop exempt, aisle kept clear
Line terminus — input only (C06), no output conveyor
